sections.title: What is a Set?
points: A set is a group of things that are clearly defined.
We use curly braces { } to list the members.
Example: { 2, 4, 6 } is a set of even numbers.

sections.title: Intersection (∩)
points: Intersection are members shared by both sets.
Example: A ∩ B contains elements that appear in A and in B.

sections.title: Union (∪)
points: Union is everything in A or B or both.
It joins all members without repeating them.

sections.title: Difference and 'Only' Regions
points: A - B means the members in A but not in B.
This is called the 'A only' region.
Example: If A = {1, 2, 3} and B = {3, 4}, then A - B = {1, 2}.

cards.fact: The overlap where both sets share members.
All members from both sets combined.
cards.mnemonic: Union is a big team of both sets.

cards.fact: Members in A that are not in B.
Sets are written with curly braces and symbols like ∩ and ∪.
cards.mnemonic: Braces hold the set, symbols show the action.
